title: Debug software program code at the level of program modules
glossary: Abstract class
A class that cannot be instantiated on its own and is intended to be a base class, often declaring abstract methods that derived classes must implement.
Breakpoint
A marker set in the source code that tells the debugger to pause program execution at that line so the program state can be inspected.
Class
A blueprint that defines the fields (state) and methods (behavior) of the objects created from it in object-oriented programming.
Debugger
A tool that controls the execution of a program, allowing step-by-step execution, breakpoints and inspection of variables to locate defects.
Encapsulation
The OOP principle of bundling data with the methods that operate on it and restricting direct access to an object's internal state.
Garbage collection
Automatic reclamation of memory occupied by objects that are no longer reachable, as implemented in languages such as Java, C# and Python.
Inheritance
A mechanism by which a class (descendant) acquires the fields and methods of another class (ancestor), enabling code reuse and hierarchies.
Interface
A contract that declares a set of methods a class must implement, supporting polymorphism and, in many languages, a form of multiple inheritance.
Object
A concrete instance of a class that holds its own state in fields and responds to messages through its methods.
Polymorphism
The ability of a single interface or method call to operate on objects of different classes, resolved through overriding or overloading.
Stack trace
A report of the active stack frames at the moment an exception or breakpoint occurs, used to trace the sequence of calls that led to an error.
Unit test
An automated test that verifies the correct behavior of a single, isolated unit of code such as a function, method or class.
